pattern: Order is irrelevant to subsequence size, so sorting ascending and taking smallest elements first lets a prefix-sum binary search answer each budget query in logarithmic time.
hints: To fit the most elements under a sum cap, you would always prefer the smallest elements first.
Sort the array, build prefix sums, and the answer for a query is how many leading elements stay within the budget.
After sorting and prefix-summing, binary search each query value in the prefix-sum array to find the largest count whose cumulative sum is at most the query.

edge_inputs.description: budget too small for any element
query equal to a prefix sum is inclusive
query just below a prefix boundary

stress.description: random array and many random budget queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    m = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    queries = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums, queries]

complexity.time: O(n log n + m log n)
